customer_anxiety_profile: Scott is a skeptical, time-constrained homeowner who has been through multiple solar evaluations with no useful outcome. His primary anxiety is that this appointment will waste his Saturday the same way prior solar meetings wasted his time. He is not hostile — he agreed to the appointment — but his commitment is shallow and conditioned on a Friday confirmation call.
unresolved_concerns: Scott's stated experience that 'none of the numbers ever came back worth shit' on prior solar evaluations — this was never addressed on the call and is the most likely reason he bails before or during the appointment.
Scott's financing question ('I'm taking out a loan against my house') was answered inconsistently by Brandon and was not resolved cleanly — Scott may still be unclear on the program structure.
No verify data was captured: bill amount, utility company, roof age, and co-decision-maker status are all unknown.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior negative solar evaluation experiences — Scott has been pitched multiple times with no useful outcome.
Financing structure skepticism — Scott probed whether this involves a loan against his house.
exact_promises_rep_made: Saturday appointment at 12:00 PM (next Saturday, the 18th).
A Friday confirmation call the day before the appointment.
The appointment will take approximately 30 minutes.
The specialist will bring a design and show how the program works.
closer_should_know: Scott's core skepticism — 'I've done a few of these and none of the numbers ever came back worth shit' — was never addressed on the setter call. The closer must open by acknowledging this directly and differentiating Ion's design process from what Scott has seen before, before showing any numbers. If the closer leads with a standard pitch, Scott will disengage within the first five minutes.
